Adding the grain seemed to elevate the salad into more of a meal (esp. if one adds some cooked chicken?) but I could imagine that some cooked barley would well and be more substantive than quinoa.  No, I did not parboil.  The vinaigrette did the trick to "cook" the broccoli as in a ceviche.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Ok, so for this boomer, is "rinsing" and "straining" a joke?  I wondered about it before I tried since my experience w/quinoa is that neither of those words work well with the grain.  I parboiled the brocco and it was fine though I will def try this as designed and prob w/an orzo or brown rice.  Unless of course the water actually does drain from the quinoa before the broc goes bad.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: OMGoodness.  I pulled the jar of CHIA instead of Quinoa!  No WONDER it was such a disaster.  Got to laugh at our crazy kitchen mistakes. oh well, it still tastes ok

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Loved this flavorful salad.  Served it as my grain and vegetable. I steamed the broccoli, per the family preference.  I was out of pecans and dried cranberries so substituted almonds and raisins.  The “sauce” was delicious and really pulled all the flavors together.  Next time I will still steam the broccoli and explore completing the recipe as written.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Used barley instead of quinoa as I prefer the chewiness and nutty flavor.  Grated cheddar because I didn't read the recipe closely enough.  Absolutely delicious!  Makes a lot.  Serves 4 lumberjacks, maybe.  8 lunch main dish servings for most adults and easily feeds 12 as a side.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Loved this.  I couldn’t find cranberries at any store, so I used  dried cherries.  I also forgot to add the Dijon mustard, still was wonderful!  I will definitely make again!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Cheddar, ugh. Better: Feta, blue(or bleu), quality parmesan, even mozz.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Did not include the mustard, seems counter-intuitive, was delicious without.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this, per directions, and it was delicious .   In the encore version, I added diced celery and pomegranate seeds, resulting in a bowl of glistening salad gems.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: There is no need to have cheddar or some kind if cheese in every vegetable dish. The sauces and dressings will suffice.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious refreshing salad.  Followed the recipe exactly.  Lasted 3 days in fridge -- tasty to the very end.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is so good and easy to make. Got me out of my cooking slump.  Filling with yummy flavor and healthy ingredients.  I did not have honey so I substituted maple syrup. I also blanched the broccoli briefly.  Will make again. And not just for Mediterranean Week.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this today and really love the flavors…but I think the recipe should be a little clearer with quantity of the quinoa. I used 1 cup of uncooked quinoa because it said how to cook the quinoa in the recipe but that is way too much when you mix it all together.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Swapped sunflower seeds for nuts due to allergy, and it was great.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this exactly as written other than I was using up some holiday spiced pecans that had been toasted with some sugar and spices, rather than plain nuts.  I was looking for a way to use up some cooked quinoa. I didn't chop the broccoli, I tore it into little florets and left out the stems. It was better than I expected. Something about the little bites of cheddar really makes it -- there's  sweetness, savoriness, creaminess and crunch. Make it, especially if you have leftover quinoa!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Yum!! I ended up doubling the dressing for the amount of broccoli and quinoa and was not unhappy that I did. Re: raw broccoli - try it! It doesn't taste raw once macerated in the dressing and retains that really nice crunch. And yes on a good aged cheddar. Levels everything up.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I really enjoyed this salad. Next time I would double the lemon.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this for a potluck where the request was to keep foods low sodium and plant-based. So I added only very little salt and replaced the cheddar with nutritional yeast. Despite these changes (especially the next to no amount of salt) it tasted quite good. I'm quite impressed with this one. Plus it's a pretty dish!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is wonderful!  The dressing is fabulous, the broccoli is perfect just as the recipe is written.  I can envision lots of substitutions, but for the first try as written it was excellent.  Colorful, fresh tasting, healthy!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Tasty! I wanted about 25 percent more dressing. Saved cheese, nuts and cranberries aside to add over the days I ate this.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this with 2x the lemon, half the honey and no apple. I think that addressed the issue with sweetness as I found it to be well-balanced and tasty. Agree with the comments regarding the number of servings this recipe makes - it will be difficult for this small household to finish this off within 3 days. I would make it again but would definitely cut the recipe in half.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Try this salad, it’s delicious. I didn’t have pecans so I used slivered almonds and sunflower seeds and it was still very good, although I think pecans would be amazing. I would not have tried this due to the raw broccoli but thanks to the comments, I’m glad I did. The finely chopped broccoli is tamed by the dressing.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Just made this - omitted the cheddar since it’s a side dish with salmon - and it’s definitely a keeper!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious!

A few modifications:
- I doubled the dressing and measured with my heart when dressing the broccoli. Definitely recommend making 2x or 3x.
- The dressing strikes me as quite sweet and quite tangy. The acid is great once everything is dressed, but there are many other sweet elements in the salad, so I'd cut the honey by 1/2 or 2/3.
- I added maybe 1/3 of a finely chopped red onion, rinsed well under cold water to remove the bite. The savory note from an allium is a must.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I'm a big fan of this recipe! The sharp cheddar was such a great addition. This kept perfectly in the fridge all week to have for lunch. Great way to get in lots of micronutrients!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this as written except halved the honey and left out the cranberries after deciding it was sweet enough with the apple.  The raw broccoli, like so many have said, is excellent.  Overall, however, this salad to me is “meh”.  I think it’s missing a bitter element to offset the sweetness.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I used farro instead of quinoa.  Added an additional lemon’s worth of juice.  This dish was outstanding.  I plain toasted my pecans and they were outstanding.  This was dinner and I couldn’t have been happier.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this tonight.  We used a pouch of President's Choice microwave quinoa, a package of shredded cheddar (vs chopping it up ourselves) and Craisins.  My husband chopped the broccoli & apple, and next time, I would probably chop it into smaller pieces than he did. The dressing is very tasty, and I almost wish there had been a little more of it. We both very much enjoyed it and will look forward to eating up the leftovers for lunch over the next day or two.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This recipe gave me mushy quinoa. Will not be using again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Followed the recipe as is - letting it sit for a few hours so the dressing saturates the broccoli made it even better. I will make this agin.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I usually spend a little time on Sunday making lunches for the week and this one was great!  It kept really well, was easy to eat at my desk and has both carbs and veggies.  (I did leave out the cheese because I didn't have any and the apple because I thought the salad would keep better without.)  I paired it with a protein and had well balanced and delicious lunches all week.  I did not par boil the broccoli but it tasted mellow and cooked with the vinegar.  Def in the meal prep rotation!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious recipe! I didn’t have apple so I substituted pear and didn’t do cheddar bc that sounded weird. Saving this for the future!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this twice now, and its on the menu again for tomorrow. No need to blanch the broccoli—really! And Im not at all a fan of raw broccoli but the dressing takes care of that. I only use half the cheese for health reason but I make sure it is really high quality sharp cheddar. Serving with roasted chicken breasts (the ATK recipe).

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I love this recipe. I upped the amount of nuts and somewhat the cheese. 
My favorite way to have this is to soft poach two eggs by cracking in a small ramekin and putting in microwave for one minute and putting on top of salad and mixing it together. So satisfying and a great breakfast.
I have also mashed avacado in it and it was delish.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this exactly as written- unusual for me. I did grate the cheese. My daughter and I liked it. Tastes healthy and a bit different. I’d like to try it with a different heartier grain. It does make a lot of salad!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent. I made it as directed - as others have noted, the broccoli didn’t taste raw after sitting in the dressing. For anyone who doesn’t have ALL the ingredients, I tasted it with just the broccoli, quinoa, and dressing and it was fabulous. The apple, cheddar and pecans are great but not necessary.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I tried this dish tonight…my observations - while I thought it was tasty, it needed a bit more dressing. So, added the other lemon half juiced and a bit more extra virgin olive oil…still thought the flavor profile was amiss. While looking through the frig produce drawer saw fresh green onions - sliced a couple up - it added a much needed zip.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I left out the cheddar & it needs something. I think I’ll try adding feta. I also used loquat fruit since I had some I needed to use. I’ll make this again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I used Granny Smith apple.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I love this salad as written, although I left out the cheese cubes as a personal preference to eat less cheese. So much texture and flavor! Thank you.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and easy to make. Followed recipe as written except 1/2 ingredients for only the two of us. Great for summer vegetarian guests.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Double the dressing for sure — raw broccoli is not pretty enough be doing all that without enough dressing! Really good with a lot of texture though.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I didn't have the cheese, or cranberries on hand but wanted to make this anyway.  I substituted raisins, and it was delicious!  Not surprised tho, I have loved all of Sohla's recipes that I've tried.    Thanks for another winner Sohla!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Great make ahead for a dinner party salad.  The video was helpful to see how she cut the broccoli.  I was skeptical about the cheddar, but it works.  I really love this salad and will enjoy mixing it up with different grains, nuts, and cheeses.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I wanted to keep this for a few days so I didn’t add the cheddar or nuts until I was eating it, when I added them directly to my serving. I think I’ll try with dates instead of cranberries next time, and maybe feta instead of cheddar.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: "Aggressive" salting amounted to 2 tsps. for me, hardly the fistful I've seen Sohia throw in on videos. It was WAAAAAY too much for us, but I was able to temper it with extra lemon. I used goat cheese and toasted walnuts, subbed fresh corn for apples b/c that's what I had on hand, added red onions. Other than the saltiness, which I'm tasting as I type, it was delish.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Wow, this is surprisingly good. Planning to use as an easy and relatively healthy lunch all week. Added a can of chickpeas for a little extra protein but I suspect diced chicken would be nice too.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Really delicious salad, and if you’re a bit iffy about raw broccoli this is the one to get you over that fear. I say that having still blanched the broccoli for about 10 seconds beforehand and ran under ice water, but it was still crunchy and lovely. Requires more salt than you think overall and do take note on the emphasis of heavily seasoning your water for the quinoa.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: My go-to take along, although all the chopping always makes it take longer to make than I think it is going to.  Leisurely day ahead with a good podcast  to make the time pass.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Subbed pistachios for pecans
Could have doubled dressing

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Needs a bit more dressing, so cut back the broccoli-quinoa to offset.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Be sure to follow the steps in order. Apples need to go in with broccoli to get coated with acid so they don't brown. I didn't do that, my apples are now brown, but I will next time. For my family's tastes, subbed pistachios and goat cheese. After putting it all together, think it may need a LITTLE more dressing. I made another half batch to add to taste or to drizzle over protein to make a main course. Super yummy!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious.  The farro I subbed for quinoa matched the heartiness of the other ingredients well.  I added a chopped HB egg for some protein which turned out to add a nice soft texture.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I was looking for a dinner that didn’t heat up the house on a very hot day and this raw broccoli salad was perfect. Quinoa cooks fast. I was tempted not to roast the pecans but, as you know, it is always worth it. Yum.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Super salad! I didn’t have any quinoa on hand so I used couscous. I also likely chopped broccoli too much (recipe says a fine chop) because I didn’t look at the posted pic, but it still tastes delicious. I agree with others who wrote that it is best to not cook the broccoli. The dressing really makes this salad pop! This is a keeper for sure.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Wish I would have added half of the quinoa. It calls for 1 cup, rinsed. But maybe I misunderstood and was supposed to measure a cup after it was cooked? Don't get me wrong, it's delicious and I like quinoa so no harm done- however it gobbled up all of the ingredients and now it just looks like quinoa is the main ingredient. Bummed. It's a HUGE batch for me and my husband, but we will eat all of it.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this with two small changes - left out the apple (didn’t have one) and only used half the honey. It is so delicious. I cook the quinoa ahead of time, making a big batch in the Instant Pot and then freezing one cup portions for future use. Will definitely make again!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
This is a perfect salad. I too was scared of the raw broccoli but the acid "cooks" it - it's super delicious this way! 
Made just as listed but second time I went a little heavy on the lemon and a lil extra cheese and nuts cause those are the fun bits. Super filling, delicious and makes really great cold lefotvers.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Great!  Checks the box for “eat more vegetables”.
- let just-purchased broccoli macerate - imho the broccoli and the Dijon vinaigrette are the only mandatory ingredients, the rest can be free-form
- subbed Asian pear for apple
- subbed toasted cashews and pepitas for pecans (toasted sunflower seeds would be great)
- subbed goji berries for cranberries
- still undecided about cheese to use … thinking about it while I finish my bowl of this salad. :)
- subbed leftover brown rice quinoa mix for quinoa

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: It needed more dressing. I would double it next time, but the flavor combination was fantastic. It's a great alternative to the recipe with mayo!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I used the whole lemon in the dressing, and substituted walnuts or almonds if I didn’t have pecans.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: It was very good.  I served chicken on top for the non-vegetarians, but like someone else’s idea of adding garbanzo beans for next time.  I will skip the cheese next time - it didn’t seem to add much and it looked odd to have the yellow cheese in the salad.  I used raisins because it was what I had, but I look forward to going with cranberries next time.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is very good, although I changed a few things to prevent a trip to the grocery store. Made the dressing as written (in the amount suggested), but subbed pine nuts for the pecans, feta for the cheddar, and dried currants for the cranberries. Also didn't have an apple. As long as you get the crunchy, salty, and sweet components, this will stay balanced. Did the quinoa hands-off in the rice cooker earlier in the day - the rest came together in less than 15 minutes.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Makes many more side servings! Don't double

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: It was lovely - I substituted farro

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I followed the recipe as written - I discovered I didn't have apple cider vinegar but I did have a bottle of boiled apple cider.  I think it was an excellent substitute.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I used one tbsp of hot honey to give it a little kick. Was perfect.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Do not rinse the quinoa.
Cook it in just over 2x the water, on slow simmer, covered, until just barely all the water is absorbed. Turn onto a cookie sheet to cool slightly and steam off. Beautiful perfect little beads with halos. Nutty and al dente chewy.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: What a surprise! Made exactly as directed - such an unusual combination of ingredients. Very easy to eat. The raw broccoli is absolutely not a problem.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I agree with other comments that it needs to be clear that the broccoli should macerate in the dressing. I also think the dressing quantity was not enough to cover the amount of broccoli requested or things in the salad. In the future, I would quickly steam the broccoli to give it a bright brain color and then macerate for at least 15 minutes before mixing with the other ingredients.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Followed the recipe almost exactly, except subbed raisins for the cranberries, because I don't like the refined sugar that is added to most dried cranberries.  Was tempted to par-boil the broccoli but so glad I did not.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: My children, who do not like quinoa, ate this happily. My husband, who does not like vegetarian food, ate this happily. What a winner

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
This tasted great with brown rice instead of quinoa, and maple syrup instead of honey. I had some sliced avocado with it.
Next time I’ll make some of Yotam Ottolenghi’s quick pickled sumac onions to add a bit more sharpness to the flavour.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
@AJR
Ooh. Yes. Pickled red onions! Thanks for the idea.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is my favorite recipe EVER!!! Makes me love love broccoli and so simple. Tonight I had it over some rigatoni for a full meal. Thank you nyt

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Very tasty.  Great base recipe to tweak to your own tastes, and a healthier dupe to the mayo based “broccoli bacon salad” that is so addictive.  Our tips: Double the dressing recipe, sub in some hot honey, needs aggressive salt/pep seasoning (as do most grain salads for us).  Leave broccoli raw, no need to blanch.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I agree with many of the reviewers of this recipe, that some of the ingredients are questionable depending on your taste.  I revised the recipe to include:  white quinoa, feta cheese, pine nuts, par-boiled broccoli mostly crowns w/stems chopped medium, 3 T chopped chives, green tart apple chopped not fine.  Dressing:  3 T olive oil,2 T sherry vinegar, one clove crushed garlic,  and pepper shaken in jar.  Add to room temp quinoa and then add other ingredients w/chives and toasted pine nuts last.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this a crucifer salad with broccoli, cauliflower and cabbage. No cranberries so used dried blueberries and chopped up apricots. Mix of nuts: pecans, walnuts and pistachios. Used about 50 g of quinoa; 60 would have been better. Make all the dressing for 2/3 of the recipe. Very tasty.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Extremely weird salad, somehow tasted pretty good regardless.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: It's delicious have made it many times.  Would not change any part of the recipe.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I'm curious if anyone has suggestions for a non-dairy cheese that might go with this or another way to add an additional protein for someone who is vegan. Thanks so much!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: @Brett quinoa and pecans are already high in protein so you can just leave the cheddar out. Or maybe add beans. Some people will suggest vegan cheese —I’m not fond of it personally, and it rarely has much protein or other nutritional value. Be aware that most vegans don’t eat honey so replace with something like maple or agave syrup.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I can only assume that Sohla El-Waylly tested this recipe to the ends of the earth because it is PERFECT!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Subbed Trader Joe’s farro for the quinoa (had on hand), bit extra cheese, whole lemon's worth of juice, otherwise as written. So tasty and easy. Chop the broccoli into small pieces, no need to cook it first!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Have made this twice, 2nd time without the quinoa and it's excellent either way.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I just made this, and it’s pretty simple and tasty, a lot like the tabouli at one of my favorite restaurants. The raw broccoli when chopped fine lends a fresh coleslaw-like texture. It has a strong tart/sour zing freshly made, though it may mellow after sitting in the fridge. I couldn’t really taste the cheddar cheese and may try it with crumbled feta next time, or just skip the cheese all together. I would consider keeping this on repeat in the summer when it’s too hot to want to cook.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Very tasty!  But, as a side dish, way too much food food for a family of 3 or 4.  Cut it in half.   For me, the cheddar is unnecessary.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this with substitutes based on what I had on hand. Basmati brown rice instead of quinoa, an feta instead if cheddar. Everything else was according to the recipe and delicious. Served it with roast chicken for a warmish early spring dinner.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Also, lightly steamed the broccoli and ran under cold water to make is just slightly tender.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe. However don't forget to rinse the quinoa, as it says, or it will be too salty.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The cheddar made this healthy dish just sing!  Made exactly to recipe.  Yum.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This makes a HUGE amount of salad (almost 14 cups worth) - great for a week and a half worth of generous lunches for 1, or a potluck. I made as written for my husband and I (delicious!) and another time with farro for a work lunch potluck (it disappeared!). It’s now in the regular rotation for plan-ahead lunches, though I half the recipe.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I cut the amount of quinoa in half and added some red onion. It is delicious and will definitely make again!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is very good.  I have made it several times. The last time I made more dressing, maybe 1/2 or 1/3 more and upped the lemon and vinegar.  I only used half of  the quinoa and after it was cooked and cooled, put it in a bowl and poured half of the dressing on it and stirred well. I dressed the rest of the ingredients with the other half of the dressing and then combined the two.  It was Excellent.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Raw broccoli chopped up with toppings is a completely different experience than eating a stalk from a raw veggie platter. Highly recommend trying it. You might get hooked (like me) and find it’s a healthy time saver for a variety of salad options all week long.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Great to make ahead of time for camping or to otherwise feed a crowd. Easy to serve the cheese and nuts on the side to accommodate allergies.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Maybe my quinoa was a little old? It just wasn’t that great for me and it seemed like a lot of work chopping up fruits and veggies for something that didn’t wow me.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Make this about twice a month for a long time now! It’s great to have in the fridge when too tired to make lunch, or as a packed lunch for the family.

It’s a great way to get in veggies and healthy grains and my husband who had never had quinoa before, loves it!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Just follow the recipe! It’s so good! Make sure to chop the broccoli very finely and you will have a fantastic lunch.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I’ve made it twice now- exactly as written but with goat cheddar instead of bovine.  
Rave reviews- took it for a neighborhood picnic where it was devoured! 
Great recipe- for again and again. 
And yes, broccoli softens in the mix- no need to precook in any way

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
IMHO, this was a big Meh. I used 1lb of raw brocc and 1/2 cup quinoa, but the dressing was inadequate. Lemon zest was excessive. Apple and nuts were nice. Sharp cheddar? No. 

I "fixed" it by adding mayo (~1/3 c) and half a chopped red onion. Gorgonzola was delish; feta would be good. 

But I'm never making this again. Gonna stick with the classic Midwestern (NOT Southern) broccoli salad: mayo/sour cream/ACV dressing, chopped celery, dried cranberries, red onion, & sunflower seeds.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is lovely. I only had candied pecans, so I omitted the cranberries. I used a granny smith apple and left out the cheese (partner is dairy free). served along with rotisserie chicken for an easy weeknight meal.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I LOVE this salad. Totally addictive. Was a little sweet for my palate. Subbed goji berries for cran (cuz they have no added sugar). Reduced honey to 1 tablespoon. Omitted the apple and cheddar actually. Added some chives, parsley, and shredded red cabbage for more punch and crunch. Took this camping and it just got better and better over several days.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: @Lisa S I agree. I doubled checked that I did everything correctly because I couldn't understand all the 5 * reviews?!?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This salad has entered into my meal prep routine on a regular basis. I save time chopping and use my food processors slicing tool for the broccoli and the apple and it makes a huge difference!!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious.  Can be made in advance which is always nice for the host.  Excellent with chicken or pork.  1. If you finely chop the broccoli and apple the salad is not as pretty as it would be if you chop according to the recipe photo.  2.  Adding extra dressing eliminates the need for added salt and pepper and lifts the flavor.  3. This recipe can only be made in 30 minutes if there are 2 cooks in the kitchen.  4.  Servings are generous.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: So good! I made the amount of dressing called for, then did another half batch of dressing, then ended up doing yet another half batch. Next time I make, I plan on doubling dressing from the beginning. Secondly, I am glad I did not parboil the broccoli. Lastly... I'm the kind of person who doesn't necessarily care about adding cheese to a salad. But with this recipe, it made a big difference (in the best way)!!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious. Made one sub:  salted,roasted pepitas for walnuts. Keeps well. Half the recipe and use full amount of dressing— unless you’re feeding a crowd.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: excellent.  don't be miserly with the salt.  Might be able to add chopped scallions. bagged shredded cheddar saved time but the time to chop to small cubes would be worth it.  bagged broccoli florets probably a time saver--I still chopped them a bit, and had to trim off a lot of dried stem ends.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this the night before for a picnic for four; put in quart baggies w/names, so each of us could take the leftovers home for another meal if we liked it - was a hit!  I thought it was a bit dry, so doubled the dressing, added half to the salad to macerate overnight.  Brought the remaining bit to the picnic; only one of us needed to add extra.  Leftovers were happily taken home by all!  This works because the broccoli is diced; no need to parboil. Will try farro next time!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The combination of flavors and textures in this salad is wonderful. Next time, I'll make two small changes: make the quinoa the night before to eliminate wetness and add the nuts to each serving right before eating to prevent soggy nuts. Marinating the broccoli in the dressing takes away the rawness, making cooking it unnecessary.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Hello all you beautiful people ! 
This is a delicious, bright, healthy salad, and keeps really well for a few days.
A little bit fussy, so not sure if we’ll do it again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this many times and prefer to use farro over quinoa. I usually skip the cranberries (stuck in teeth!) and find it’s still tasty without. Use whatever nuts I have in the pantry. Pecans are great

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!! Enough salad for an army, or at least a platoon. We served it as a side to 6 people and had lots left over. Since it easily could be cut in half, adjust your recipe accordingly.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is on heavy rotation at my house!  I replace with dried cherries, pistachios, and aged, crumbly cheddar, because that's what we keep on hand. I can eat the entire bowl in one sitting. Better the next day -- gives the raw broccoli time to mellow.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was pretty good and definitely healthy, but the taste is a little bland. I added a little aged white balsamic and it tasted better. Also, like someone else suggested I feel like Feta would be better than Sharp Cheddar as the Cheddar kind of got lost in flavor.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Do NOT parboil the broccoli! If you’re concerned it won’t be tender or flavorful enough, just chop it smaller - into a dice. The commenters who have said that the broccoli “cooks” in the acid of the dressing are 100% correct - soften but remains toothy. This would not be nearly as delicious with cooked broccoli. Made it to the recipe, we devoured it (teenagers included) and ro-sham-bo’d to decide who would get leftovers for lunch tomorrow…

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: @MsMcK I seriously agree.  Have made and loved this recipe multiple times.  Today, decided to blanch broccoli (45 seconds) as I have found that leftovers are a bit washed out looking the next day.  I already regret my decision…

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: As the salad marinates, the broccoli releases some water and also “cooks” in the vinaigrette turning a nice green.  I upped the pecans and cheese a bit but it’s excellent as is. I have also made this with a little bit of chipotle in adobo added to the dressing  which adds a touch to Smokey heat.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: So the broccoli are raw?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Yes. That's normal for a fresh salad.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Is the cheddar absolutely necessary? I'm strongly considering leaving it out - just doesn't sound like something that would go well with the other ingredients :/

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The cheddar is what tipped the balance for me and convinced me to add this to this week's meal list! But of course it isn't 100% necessary--the recipe police won't come to your house and arrest you if you leave it out. Or maybe you could replace it with crumbled feta...that sounds pretty good too.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Cheddar goes well with broccoli in soup so maybe? I agree with the other suggestion about feta, or if you aren't averse to it crumbled blue might be nice.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I don’t care for mustard. Any good substitutes?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Maybe try some light miso and add a little more vinegar and or lemon juice?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Excellent salad. I subbed cooked kamut for the quinoa and doubled the dressing amounts.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: If you look closely at the image, they present, the broccoli is not raw

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Recipe is pretty good, but what a ridiculous and unnecessary name.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this tonight and absolutely loved it. I try all sorts of dressings and this one is a delight. I used a nice crisp tart apple, good sharp cheddar and good quality honey. I also steamed my broccoli a bit because that is how we like it in salads.  And I only had chopped dried cherries but would have used cranberries. You can make this exactly as per the recipe and you will enjoy it greatly I promise. Simple but really nice recipe!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Raw Vs steamed?  I make the Italian Broccoli salad often. The simple oil and vinegar dressing softens the raw broccoli as it sits. I always make a day ahead. Keeps getting better! It calls for provolone cheese. I cannot like cheese with in that salad tried three tries but I add more almonds and pepperonci 
add more almonds and peperoncini. The

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Is it one cup of cooked quinoa? Or all the quinoa from one cup uncooked? That’s too much quinoa.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: It is one cup of uncooked quinoa. Instructions for cooking the quinoa are in the first paragraph of the recipe.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this today, exquisite. I love broccoli, and this is an incredibly tasty way to enjoy it. I did follow one reader's comment and blanched the broccoli ever so slightly, and I preferred it this way. This will become a staple in our kitchen!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The recipe calls for one cup of quinoa, cooked or uncooked. Big difference!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The directions for cooking the quinoa are in the first paragraph of the recipe.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Substituted walnuts for pecans, finely grated pecorino for cubed cheddar, maple syrup for honey.  Didn’t have anything to sub for dried cranberries so left them out. Consider adding chopped fennel stalks next time. Super tasty, will definitely make again and can imagine many variations on this theme. Team raw broccoli and dressing quantity as written!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I prepared this today and am generous giving it four stars.  Of course there were some changes.  Brown rice instead of quinoa, blanched the broccoli, no cheddar which is not my preference, and I added some Aleppo pepper, too.  I found the dish sort of bland/not enough flavor.  Next time, I’d farro which is more toothsome than rice, add more nuts, cranberries, and Aleppo pepper, feta, too.  I wanted to like this as a way to eat more healthy broccoli, but the dish didn’t ring the bell.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was absolutely delicious as-is (except I didn’t have the dried cranberries and they’d give the sweet piece that I was missing). Five stars! I’ll take it for lunch this week.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Could I use sunflower seeds instead of pecans (need to avoid nuts)?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: sure!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: don't normally like dried fruit in vegetable salads, but lately see it a lot. How's this salad without the dried cranberries, anyone tried this without the dried cranberries? How was it?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I'm not a big quinoa fan, though I'll give this a try. What might I use instead? Rice seems too mushy and bland.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is definitely a keeper. I made it on a sub-zero night so blanched the broccoli and added the quinoa before it cooled. Used dried cherries instead of cranberries and chopped ups green onion that needed using. It’s a starch and salad in one bowl! A perfect accompaniment to salmon or chicken, or as a veggie stand alone.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: very tasty.  it seems like it would make a lot, so i cut the recipe in half for the first try.  i used 15-year cheddar - the tartness and cheese crystals added a lot, I thought.  next time I will try with toasted walnuts or toasted hazelnuts.  (I have a whole drawer of cheeses so could switch that out, too.  aged swiss?)

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I always rinse my quinoa before cooking to eliminate the bitter outer coating. Otherwise this recipe is perfect!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I didn't have quinoa so substituted pearl couscous and still delicious!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is the best salad I have made in a while. A , would recommend. Also makes a ton so good for leftovers

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I inadvertently left out the apple and then added it afterward and preferred it without. Perhaps because I used leftover steamed broccoli instead of raw and subbed sliced almonds for the pecans since that's what I had on hand. It was a terrific lunch and a nice change of pace.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Just made this for dinner after reading the recipe.  Followed directions.  Flavors will meld together as it sits in refrigerator for dinner.   Pairing it with Perfectly Roasted Pork Tenderloin and golden beets.  
Of course, I had to sample it before placing in refrigerator.  It is delicious! Will put in my rotation recipes.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: What is the best way to toast the almonds for this recipe?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I toasted the pecans (not almonds) on the stovetop in a small pan on low heat. Works great! Just don't forget about them — stir occasionally, and try to notice when they start to become fragrant. Good luck!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Manchego cheese works if you're out of cheddar! Also added more lemon juice at the end, but otherwise made as written.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Everyone here from the South knows that raw broccoli an a salad like this is delicious. Find it at every church function, birthday party and potluck. Definitely making this version soon!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Cheddar?  I use Feta?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is a really good salad -- nice mix of textures and flavors. I will happily make and serve this again, as is.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Thought this was very good! I made it pretty much as specified in the recipe and it was delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this exactly as directed and it is wonderful! Looking forward to seeing how the leftovers improve with time. Definitely do NOT parboil the broccoli. The raw crunch mellows as it sits but still maintains a nice bite.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: raw broccoli is not appealing. I always briefly parboil chopped broccoli then plunge in ice water to stop the cooking if I plan to use it in a salad. Not only does this improve the flavor, it brightens the color to a very pretty and palatable bright green.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Wow, this was great! I subbed pistachios for the pecans and parboiled the broccoli. Otherwise, followed faithfully. A delicious result. Loved.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this with bulgur and added a handful of mint. - delicious !

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Awesome recipe - a complete meal! Made it as written; had everything on hand except pecans so I subbed walnuts. Like another reviewer, I too used a bit less quinoa; nicely balanced. If you get a fresh head of broccoli and chop it up fairly small, there are no issues with it being raw. It somewhat “cooks” in the delicious dressing. 
A definite keeper. Thanks Melissa!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I roasted the broccoli. It added great depth.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this yesterday and I have had three servings already. Added shredded carrots as well and cooked the broccoli for one minute prior to chopping. Next time will make with feta instead of cheddar. Squeezed half a lemon over it the second day. Delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This works well with millet.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious. I did blanch the broccoli because I was nervous about so much raw broccoli. After reading some of the notes, I will try this again using raw broccoli. I think it would make a great side dish to bring to a party. Can’t wait to share it with friends and family!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Loved this: chopped broccoli VERY fine; had to substitute yoghurt for cheddar. Great recipe.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Calories? Nutritional information?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was a great weeknight meal, especially when you cook the quinoa in the Inastapot (thank you to the reader who mentioned that option). I followed the recipe but used vegan cheddar shreds and added some chopped smoked turkey to make it more of a meal

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I was super dubious about the cheese but it’s amazing. Seemed a bit dry so added the juice of the other half lemon and an extra tbs cider vinegar. Perfect! This is a great meal-prep or lunchbox recipe. 

I did pre-steam the broccoli briefly because of someone’s digestion issues. I think it would be great either way.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I used mostly honey roasted pecans wi the a sugary coating, and some almonds for the rest of the 3/4 c of nuts. So good! And used a mix of dried cherries and cranberries for the dried fruit.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was very delicious  I followed all directions and only used sliced toasted almonds for pecans.  I will make this often -it would be very convenient to take on picnics. The broccoli is chopped fine and does not need any cooking- it was very tender

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I just made this and it is DELICIOUS! Didn't have enough broccoli so added chopped kale, sprouted brown rice (no quinoa on hand) forgot to add the cheese, which is really not needed. Bright,  fresh,  tasty, and good for you! A Keeper Recipe; am printing it out as we speak!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I really liked it, and would definitely add the extra lemon. I had some quick pickled red onions in the fridge, and they were a good addition too.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I swapped out the quinoa for barley, the cranberries for golden raisins, and added a thinly sliced red onion. Also a generous pinch of black pepper! Tastes amazing :)

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was very, very good. However, whole lot of chopping is involved. I used my old faithful Cuisinart to chop the broccoli, apple, and nuts, and it worked out fine. I also used couscous because I did not have quinoa. The salad was delicious and perfect for a buffet. I will certainly make again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Dressing is yummy, but there’s not enough of it. This is a huge salad, and I could have used at least 50% more dressing. As is, the salad ends up tasting bland because it’s under-dressed. Adding more lemon helped, but not enough.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Had high hopes for this and it was so easy to make, but it just tasted bad. Didn’t think it tasted good the citrus and quinoa was just not it.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: What a refreshing salad on a cold winter day. I made mostly as directed, added the extra half of lemon directly to the vinaigrette. I subbed dried cherries and pistachios because that’s what I had. Will definitely make this again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this today - but only 1/2 recipe as I live alone.  The only substitution I made was using chopped dried apricots for the dried cranberries.  I do like raw broccoli, but with all of the other ingredients and the dressing you might enjoy it even if you don't.  I had a small bowl this afternoon and thought it was really good!  And I bet it will be even better tomorrow.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I wanted to like this salad. There are too many ingredients that do not go well together, why the cheese? I won’t make this again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Didn’t like it.  A little too mustardy  All the ingredients were so small their taste blended to be tasteless.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Loved it. Do not cook the broccoli! The small pieces, along with the apples give a nice crunch to the salad.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
This recipe (sans the cheddar) seems a bit like a wonderful salad the Fairway Market on 74th Street used to sell. It was very popular, but unfortunately  they discontinued it a few months ago.

Although I love fresh veggies, broccoli is one vegetable that I sometimes prefer frozen, particularly when roasting. However this recipe seems like it requires raw broccoli.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Chopping the broccoli and adding the dressing takes away the raw flavor. Needed additional acid, so I added a couple glugs of rice vinegar.  Still a bit bland, so I topped the salad with crumbled feta.  Lasted a couple days in the fridge.  This recipe is a great base and highly adaptable to tastes and ingredients on hand.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: made just as directed - parboiled the broccoli, (barely) the second time and it made it even better!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious.  I added more salt and pepper and lemon juice

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This salad makes me want to eat quinoa again. Even my husband wants to eat quinoa. Found the salad better without the cheese. The flavour of the dressing is enough and is excellent.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I also blanched the broccoli as I don’t like it raw despite the vinaigrette. I had cooked quinoa and added two cups as well as a drained and rinsed can of white beans for a bit more protein-y substance. Less honey too. Totally delicious for lunch.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made the full recipe for myself and ate it for lunch all week (added shredded chicken for some extra protein). It just got better every day! Will definitely keep this in the rotation.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: It is better the next day.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is fine as it goes but I was disappointed to find it didn't come together into a complex whole but was rather just a bowl full of bits of this and that.  Marginally better on the second day.  Perhaps adding quite a lot of grated cheddar would pull it all together.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Cut the recipe as we are just two. Delicious, a hit with hubby, who said "This is great. Please make it again."!!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I was very happy to discover that I already had almost all ingredients. It was too cold to go to the store for the cranberries though. It was really yummy even without them. Great way to eat some raw veggie.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Very good. I added bacon the 2nd day. I’ll make it again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I leave the pecans until serving time as I like my nuts crunchy, not soggy.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Awesome vegetarian entree or side dish.  I steamed the broccoli before chopping in food processor.  This makes a LOT.  would be great to bring to a pot luck.  This recipe is a keeper!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: very good

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Definitely gets zestier in the refrigerator overnight. Versatile for those who choose not to add all the toppings. Got rave reviews and will for sure make it again! Thank you for this healthy option.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I have had other similar salads that I have enjoyed but unfortunately enjoyment was not to be had with this one. I think it was probably the dressing that was to blame. Or maybe the raw broccoli. My husband did not finish the small serving I put on his plate, and though I ate my larger serving, I wish I hadn’t. All of the salad ingredients make sense but maybe just another dressing and lightly steam the broccoli? Gave me gurgly stomach all night.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent. I used farro in place of quinoa and it was great. Other than that, I wouldn't change anything.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Loved this! I cut the cheese into pea size squares, and it was plenty. Very delicious, it makes a lot, but I am sure we will eat it all by tomorrow.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I cut recipe in half and made as directed. After eating a sering there is enough for 2-3 more. Delicious. Dressing has a tangy and sweetness about it, No need to parboil the brocolli. Will add more cheddar to the next servings. An easy recipe and so delightful!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I had to substitute wild rice due to my quinoa sensitivity - this was fantastic! Perfect as a summer side. I did take one reviewers suggestion to blanche the broccoli, and I loved how bright it looked. My family loved this, and I plan to make it again!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Is this good the next day?  Was thinking of making it ahead-- would it be good cold?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious just the way it is!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Loved the recipe although I had less broccoli on hand so my final mixture was more quinoa based. I would suggest adding fresh parsley to the recipe to give it a bit more freshness and double the dressing recipe. Its terrific and you'll want more of it.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: made with wheat berries as a sub for quinoa.  Delicious.  A perfect dish to take to a party/dinner/BBQ as you can easily make this a day ahead.  (DO NOT BLANCHE THE BROCCOLI)

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Followed the recipe exactly. Does not have the 'raw brocolli' taste if the salad is mixed fully with the dressing. No complaints, it's a good salad!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made half the recipe. Even without the cheese ( don’t eat) and dried cranberries ( don’t have), it was huge - enough for 8 or 10 servings. I don’t think it can be consumed in 3 days! As for the taste, it didn’t knock my socks off but I hope it will improve with a little marinating time.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was fabulous.  I added two preserved lemons and a tablespoon of chili crisp.  In my enthusiasm, I accidentally over salted the salad (likely due to the preserved lemons), so I balanced it with two tablespoons of sugar.  We preferred the slightly sweeter version.  I will make this often but will change the cheese.  Cheddar didn't seem quite right, so I think I'll try a mild feta or queso fresco next time.  Do not parboil as others have suggested.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Can't tell you how delicious this is!  Made it with farro because I was out of quinoa, and it is delicious and keeps very well.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I followed the recipe as written.  Delicious!  Salad kept for three days and was a hit with the family.  Perfect for a summer picnic or cold winter day like today.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Is the 1 C quinoa measure for the dried quinoa before cooking; or 1C cooked quinoa. A bit unclear.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is one cup uncooked quinoa.  You can cook it according to the package instructions, let it cool, then add the cooked quinoa to the broccoli mixture.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
This was a delicious salad. Fast, easy, and the mix of tart with sweet and nuts was perfect. Followed the recipe with no diversions.  The acids worked great on raw broccoli. Soo happy with this recipe. Do I have to share? I’m
Eating it as I wait for my chicken to cook… it might be gone before that’s finished!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Very easy and fresh weeknight meal. Agree that you can take or leave the cheese. I didn’t have pecans so I threw in some toasted pepita seeds for texture. Really bright, healthy, and fresh. Would definitely make again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this with farro instead of quinoa because I had it on hand.  Higher fiber content and chewier grain.  Also used 1 & 1/2 TBSP of honey because that's all I had left!  SO GOOD!  Hearty, tasty, filling and healthy entree salad!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is a great salad. I doubled the dressing because it calls for a lot of quinoa, which I find soaks it up. I bought preshredded cheddar, which wasn't as flavorful as a nicer cheese would have been. Next time, I'll go for something better with more bite and probably use less quinoa. Otherwise I wouldn't change a thing.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
We loved this. I did roast the broccoli first then chopped. Not a fan of raw broccoli.

My husband wants to try it with buckwheat groats next time.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Got started on this, made dressing and quinoa.  Decided to cut recipe in half for two people, subbed sliced almonds (untoasted) and raisins, as what were available.  It was ok but maybe better the next day.  Can see where toasted pecans and dried cranberries would add something.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This salad got better with a few days in the fridge and the addition of our family garlic/tarragon vinegar salad dressing. Without the dressing for moisture and flavor, a bit bland.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Yummy. I’ll probably add bacon pieces to the leftovers, and then it will be perfect.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this and used bulgar for the grain. This made a huge amount of salad. It's great but it serves way more than 6.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: A bright and satisfying meal!  And I do mean meal, I used farro instead of quinoa because that’s what we had in house (trying to “shop” my own pantry lately). Also substituted roasted cashews for pecans, but only by sprinkling on top with each serving, because we like the crunch.  I would maybe add the whole lemon’s juice next time. And I would definitely encourage using the raw broccoli chopped very finely, the texture is just a really nice crunch. Nice summery salad in the middle of winter!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Feeds four? This salad is so good I could eat the entire bowl myself.  Actually, there is no end of substitutions one could make. I happened to have a large container of cooked bulgur and quinoa on hand so I used  that. I think farro would be equally excellent.  The broccoli does not at all taste raw.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This salad is surprisingly delish!  Its a keeper!  Even before adding the apple and cheese it was absolutely tasty.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I rated this recipe 3 Star because I have never made quiona before.  I followed the not so great directions... in STEP 1.  Did what it said and had mushy quionoa.  So, I looked on quiona bag directions that is specific about amount of water and used them.  So much better.  Also the recipe ingredients should say 1 cup quinoa, COOKED and rinsed.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I've made this 'spoon salad' twice and like it very much. However, it produces an enormous salad, much more than 4 - 6 portions, more like 8 - 10. Just sayin'!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Very nice; balanced.  The cheddar and toasted pecans are especially interesting.  Can think of a lot of additions and tweaks, but it’s great as-is.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Mint
Parsley
More lemon

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I love this salad. It has a great taste, is easy to make and holds well. The apples add a nice flavor.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: So easy, delicious and filling — loved how it was all mixed in the same bowl. Agree with some of the other notes that more dressing wouldn’t hurt! This isn’t a critique of the recipe but I used average-quality cheddar and springing for fancier cheese would’ve made it even better. I could see adding golden raisins or grilled chicken, too.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Healthy, easy, but not very tasty.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was great! My hack was pearl couscous. I chopped the broccoli into small pieces and let the dressing work on the raw broccoli for about 3 hours, then cubed extra sharp cheddar.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this twice now, once with cashews and once with pecans. I eat broccoli because it’s good for me, but don’t really like it. This salad is a tasty fresh and brilliant way to get your broccoli! Chop it very fine, and as others have said, drop it into the dressing while you get on with the rest. Definitely include the cheese and the cranberries, as well as the apple. Use any nuts you have to hand. It needs the lemon. It keeps really well so great for lunch boxes over several days.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: An excellent main course salad. It's a lot of laborious chopping, but it makes for prodigious amount of food, and even when dressed, the salad lasts quite well.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I loved this salad even though I had never cooked quinoa and didn't know I needed a fine mesh strainer LOL.  I made the biggest mess I have ever made without my granddaughter. I made it exactly as written only with a little less quinoa.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Interesting flavors but absolutely not worth the repetitive stress injury from finely chopping all that broccoli.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and so easy and fast! Very flavorful. Agree with others for us this is more of a side than main dish- we had with roasted salmon. But looking forward to having with lunch this week too!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is a great, make ahead lunch recipe for the week…however I *highly* recommend doubling the dressing recipe—it’s just too dry otherwise. Oh, and I second what someone else said about using the Trader Joe’s Unexpected Cheddar!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is a great fall/winter salad. I do enjoy raw broccoli, but this time I chopped it very finely so that it could macerate in the dressing. In fact, I chopped everything for this salad very finely. I was lacking a few ingredients and used Whole Foods Super Grains instead of quinoa, pineapple vinegar and dried lemon peel for the lemon, and both cheddar and swiss cheese. Oh, and dried cherries for the cranberries--I get "no sugar added" dried cherries from Michigan. Otherwise exactly as written!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The kiddo doesn’t like quinoa so made with farro - so tasty. Made a second time, still with farro, but riffed on the dressing using miso etc. such a tasty, easy and versatile dinner!!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: What a terrific salad! I, too, I am not a fan of raw broccoli, but this salad is absolutely delicious. I did find that I needed to make a little extra dressing. I also prefer to grate the cheese on top of individual servings.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I used faro instead of quinoa, and loved the nutty chew that it provided. I only had 12oz of broccoli on hand so I stretched it by adding a can of rinsed chickpeas. Great recipe. Very filling and satisfying for a winter lunch.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was great and super easy to put together. I did have to make a few substitute rooms because I didn’t have the exact ingredients, but found it to be stellar anyway! I didn’t have lemons so I used a cara cara orange and I didn’t have block cheddar so I used shredded cheese. Lastly, I didn’t have pecans, so I used toasted almond slices. It’s nice to know it’s very forgiving on the subs. My husband was impressed and can’t stop eating it. Love getting a nice dose of protein in my salad!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Amazingly delicious!  Made it precisely as directed using extra sharp cheddar (very small cubes) and it was awesome. Will add this to our repertoire.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I forgot to buy broccoli so I substituted shaved Brussels sprouts and it was delicious!
Great recipe!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: all the textures here are perfect! the dressing perfectly tempers the raw broccoli. this would be really good with other grains, too!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Attractive & versatile dish. I made it per recipe except 1) used dried cherries instead of cranberries (all I had - cut the larger ones in half); 2) used mozzarella (all I had - not a fan of cheddar); 3) stirred in some nutritional yeast as a nod to the missing cheddar. Next time (and there will definitely be one) I'll omit cheese and cheese sub altogether. I'm always looking for easy, nutritious side dishes that also work as main entree for family vegetarians and this fits the bill.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this twice now and think it’s a very tasty, healthy feeling salad. I steamed the broccoli just until bright green and only use half a cup of quinoa. Easy and tasty!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this with brown rice (but without Cheddar because I forgot!) and loved it. I'd never had broccoli raw before, it's delicious. I'll definitely do it more.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious, but needs more dressing: I’ll try 1 1/2 times dressing next time.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious salad and thought the raw broccoli worked perfectly once marinaded in the dressing. Was worried about insufficient dressing per comments so did heaping measurements. Don’t recommend for the Dijon as it became a dominant flavour with too much heat.  Otherwise, I thought the ratios in the recipe worked perfectly.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Very tasty! Subbed raisins for the dried cranberries (it’s what I had) so added more lemon juice to keep things tart. Also had some fresh mint on hand and mixed that in—was a nice addition.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This sounded so good but the “aggressively salted” quinoa made the whole dish way too salty.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I had it with Farro. Amazing,even though I hate raw broccoli in general!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: very good, though could use an herb—parsley, arugula, or sliced green onion maybe. added flax seeds.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was super yummy, make a LOT, and is very filling! The only thing I had to swap was Parmesan cheese for the cheddar and golden raisins for the cranberries, but otherwise I followed the recipe and it worked out great. The raw broccoli softened overnight and was the perfect crunchy texture by the time I ate it for lunch the next day. Will definitely be adding this into my rotation!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was really good! I decided to roast the broccoli and used a mystery grain from my pantry (hulled millet?). Next time I will double the dressing because it was so yummy.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I have made this recipe with couscous and with quinoa. l liked it better with couscous. The texture was more chewy than mushy.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made exact to recipe. Served the next day. I would not serve this immediately after making. It improves with a bit of time.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The idea of the salad and ingredients sounded great, but for me there was a bit much going on. My husband really liked it yet I found the mustard to be too forward, as well as the dressing too tart. I think if I were to make it again, I would eliminate or reduce the mustard and reduce the lemon. Loved the broccoli, nuts, cheese, and quinoa, and would try different varieties and combinations of nuts, cheese, grain, and dried fruit to mix it up.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this exactly as written and it’s delicious. The broccoli did soften a bit after sitting for a while and lost that totally raw texture. Would definitely make again and wouldn’t bother cooking the broccoli.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: LOVED LOVED LOVED this! Only had a few dried cranberries left, so subbed in currants for the remainder of the dried fruit (thought they worked fine).
