Took others' advice and doubled the dressing (loved how tangy it was); used 1/2-2/3 of it on top of what was called for. Next time I might use more apple (with red peel for contrast; this time I used a Granny Smith). I think this would go well at a pot-luck, as it made a LOT. Good thing my 17yo son likes it! :-)

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Add Feta cheese at serving

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Love this recipe. It really doesn't feel/taste like raw broccoli. Bonus: it's prepared with one bowl, one measuring cup, one measuring spoon. The chopping took a while but clean up was easy!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this exactly per the instructions and really liked the flavors a lot (and agree with everyone who said that cooking the broccoli is not necessary). I am, however, still trying to figure out (not for lack of effort on my part) what quinoa brings to the table. I think I'll make this again but will substitute farro for the quinoa.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Thank you Sohla! I've been looking for long lasting salads with ingredients I'm likely to have around. This is going to be on my WFH rotation. I like the way Quinoa gets lodged into the broccoli florets. With each chomp you get a heat of the mustard, crunch and sweetness of the apple, chewyness of the cranberry and cheese. Thank you so much. Been running out of things to eat while I work from home.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Love this salad.  Has become a “gamer salad”. Everyone I’ve made this for that is cool with broccoli has asked me for the recipe!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Pretty good.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: We used Farro and it was great.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this several times & would like to have a bowl right now!  Good potluck dish, stays tasty for several days, healthy—you can’t go wrong.  The only changes I made is to skip the lemon zest but increase the juice.  I also halved the amount of quinoa.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I like this recipe but also nit a fan of raw broccoli. Zapped it in the microwave for 2 minutes with a little water, then cooled and drained. On hand substitutions: dried cherries and ricotta salata. Added chopped scallions.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: To me, this is super tasty! Like others said, this is in no way a 6 serving situation. But I’m fine with that as I’m always on the lookout for vegetarian potluck recipes. I cooked the quinoa in 2 cups of water, seasoned with Better than Bouillon vegetable base as per brand instructions, until the quinoa was done and the water evaporated. Used juice from the whole lemon.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Endlessly riff-able! I’ve had it as written and enjoyed (perhaps didn’t love the 3rd day texture of the cheddar). Made it recently with Parmesan instead and that held up better (chopped it into tiny nubs), subbed dried cherries for the cranberries and toasted walnuts for the pecans. Love it!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Took to a party and the bowl was empty after nearly everyone took seconds.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was so good!  I used pumpkin seeds instead of pecans, because that's what I had on hand, and it was very good.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Left out the cheese and honey. Bumped up the amount of oil and vinegar to compensate. Also added 1/2 Tbs of grainy mustard.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I was intrigued by this recipe but feared it would be tough on the digestive system…have no fear, it’s delicious! Use the sharpest cheese you can find, and Granny Smith apples to balance. It makes a lot but keeps well.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Add dill

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made for a Memorial Day celebration and left broccoli raw and generally got good feedback. I would not parboil as others recommend. It’s good, but will add fresh herbs and/or thinly sliced shallot in the future

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made as written and it was delicious.  Next time I going to try feta in place of cheddar.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Yum!  I would 1 1/2 times the dressing next time.  I love raw broccoli and it does soften up when added to the dressing.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This recipe was amazing and easy. Yum.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The star here is the dressing. Made it to a tee. Fantastic dish.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this recipe exactly as stated, and it is awesome. Lasted multiple days in the fridge for lunches. I kept the Cheddar, cranberries, and pecans separate and just added them to the single serving dish each day so they wouldn't get soggy or slimy. This will definitely be added to the lunch rotation! (Don't cook the broccoli!)

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I think this salad would’ve been perfect without the cheddar or maybe with a different kind of cheese

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: A surprising and excellent salad.  The honey mustard dressing pairing with the raw broccoli is inspired.  It makes a lot of salad.  Perfect for a hot day. I will make it again but will probably skip the cheese- it didn’t feel necessary- and I might skip the quinoa too

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This salad is perfection. So easy to make, healthy and delicious. A great lunch meal. I even stopped adding the cheese because it doesn't really need it.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made as is using a Pink Lady apple and Sharp White Cheddar from Tillamook. Topped with Morningstar Chickn strips and it was a good, filling dinner. Not a typical recipe for me, but I trust Sohla’s recipe testing!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This salad is so good.  I chose to not add the cheese.  I did not cook the broccoli.  I've made this four times already.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Outstanding. Used maple syrup instead of honey, doubled the dressing and the broccoli, slivered almonds. Perfect lunch meal prep for hot summer days.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: My husband and I really liked this salad. I made 1/2 the recipe and had plenty for the two of us. I wouldn’t recommend parboiling the broccoli, the flavor of the raw broccoli is brighter and you’d lose some of the delightful crunch. My husband wants more cheese and more pecans, but I think it’s perfect as it is. The dressing would be great on any number of fruit or vegetable salads.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is my go to salad, and I eat it for days. The dressing is to die for

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Endlessly adaptable depending on what you have on hand. Makes a perfect meal prep recipe because it keeps so well. Was skeptic about the raw broccoli but it's great. Definitely a keeper!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Has anyone tried using a different grain? Maybe farro?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: An all-time favorite. Perfect for days when you do not want to heat up the kitchen for anything.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’ve watched Sohla El-Waylly since her time at the test kitchen and time and time again she makes amazing recipes with ingredient combinations I would’ve never tried before. Only change I made was that I boiled the broccoli and this meal was great!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Fresh and easy - the perfect ball to school picnic salad!  People were loving this, and many wanted the recipe.  I doubled it with no problem, and one family took it home in a ziploc bag!  I didn’t have the cranberries, so I used dried cherries (cut in half), and it was a “no nuts” picnic, so I omitted the pecans. So good, I can’t wait to make it again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this for a few friends and am going to make it for a very large dinner party this weekend, it's fantastic!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Thanks Sohla for setting the foundation for these flavors. My cooking creativity led me to use the instant pot and oven. 1 cup rinsed quinoa goes into the instant pot with 1 1/4 cups water. Cook on high for 1 minute. Let sit for 15 as steam naturally releases. I enjoy adding turmeric and black pepper for a gentle flavor and nutrition boost while the grains cook. Additionally I roasted the broccoli and added some sweet potato. Cheers to a satisfying bowl of goodness!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Super good and easy to make. Do NOT par-cook the broccoli. You can do little add-ins here on things that you like - I added some chopped dates, which gives it some nice sweetness. Once it sits overnight you will need to add more oil vinegar because those seem to soak in and be less prevalent the next day, but holds up great over a few days in the fridge.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: What could be substituted for the Dijon mustard? Grated ginger root or Horseradish?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious salad, I wouldn’t put as much quinoa in the next time.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this last night and it was delicious. I chopped the broccoli into very small pieces. I loved the flavor of the apple with the honey mustard dressing.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: As much as I try, just don't like raw broccoli, however after a quick blanch in boiling water and then an Ice water plunge and drain, It works much better in my very humble opinion.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Used barley and raisins instead of quinoa and cranberries, they were what I had and worked well.  Reluctantly left out the cheese as I'm in need of iron, some excellent sources in this but dairy inhibits iron absorption.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Changed a few things for my taste but loved this. Dressing amazing with broccoli/apple/cranberry combo

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Just made it! Sent a pic to my foodie friends and a recipe link. Looking forward to it tasting even better after it sits awhile. Used Farro as had it on hand. Otherwise I followed the recipe and forsee this being on a regular rotation. None of the ingredients go out of season for the most part. Agree that the cheddar really sells this as well as toasted pecans, and, the dressing! It's delicious!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Like that the dressing is so unique.  Made in Thursday and it held up well over the weekend for good snacking.  All that broccoli chopping was a bit tiresome, but worth it.  Made a faro since we don’t like quinoa

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made the dressing per the recipe, but then subbed the mix-ins for what I had on hand (excluded apple and pecans, added carrots, cashews, and raisins). It turned out great! I think the core quinoa, broccoli, and dressing are all you need and you can experiment from there. A versatile salad :)

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Would this work with raisins instead of dried cranberries?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: You could try it- it depends on your taste preferences. I like salads to be really tart rather than sweet (this salad as is wasn't tart enough for me), so I wouldn't sub, but if that isn't your taste preference, and you like sweet rather than tart, it would probably be fine.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Make it a day ahead. The broccoli needs to marinate and “cook” in the vinegar and lemon.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I salted the water aggressively as was instructed and the quinoa came out way too salty.  An exact measurement would be a helpful guideline. Unfortunately I used quinoa that was passed the expiration date and it was too hard! All the other flavors and textures were good, even with subbing tart cherries and a different cheese. For the recipe ingredients. I will try this again with less salt and fresh quinoa!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Perfect amount of dressing (to our taste) is 1.5X that given in the recipe.  2X is a little too much (but still delicious!)

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is such a good dish, bright & flavorful! Follow the recipe and don’t blanche the broccoli. I used farro as I thought that matched the size of the other elements a bit better than quinoa.  I skipped the cheddar as I didn’t have any,  I thought it was so delicious and I’ll make it again this week

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I added a marinated cooked and chopped chicken breast to it that I had in the fridge and it was even better!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I omitted the quinoa.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is outstanding!  Lots of chopping, but so many healthy and tasty ingredients, it's my go to potluck dish and I always bring copies of the recipe to share knowing I'll be asked!  Have made it many times.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was good! I steamed the broccoli and used goat cheese crumbles instead of cheddar. Also think it would taste better with just 2 Tbsp of Dijon. Will make it again!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Shelley…yes, I made this today with farro. 
It was delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
LOVED THIS!  Easy and healthy.
Made with what was on hand - bag of frozen broccoli (steamed in bag), no apple, no dried berries, barley instead of quinoa.  Made the vinaigrette as directed.  Absolutely delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I love this salad. I had something similar in an Indian restaurant in London. That version did not include quinoa and the dressing was more mint than mustard but the general idea of raw broccoli “cured” in a vinaigrette with nuts and dried fruit - a fresh chili is nice too - is pure crunchy delightful deliciousness. I like it equally well with it without the cheese addition so I think you can suit your own tastes and diet preferences there. Make on Sunday night and have a healthy lunch all week!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious as written! It makes a ton of food too. I halved the recipe with success. Perfect as a make-ahead lunch for the week following all the rich Thanksgiving foods.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Very nice recipe. I think it needs a bit more of the lovely dressing...about 1.5x more. Zingy cranberries are nice, but dried cherries are also really nice in this salad. Also delicious without the cheese.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I suggest reserving some of the dressing and adding it as needed at the end.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: My husband and I loved this salad! Made it on a Thursday night when we knew we should have a vegetable. I am so glad I made it exactly as written. Just make sure you use a nice cheddar that’s been aged a bit. I had to supplement with a softer cheddar (someone had been snacking on the reserve cheddar) and the softer cheddar pieces don’t hold their form as well. Regardless I loved the cubed cheddar, it felt so special.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Just checking - is it 1 cup of quinoa before cooking or after?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Followed exactly except subbed sun dried tomatoes for the cranberries because that's what was available, and it worked nicely. Finely chopped broccoli did not taste or feel raw at all.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The second time that I made this recipe I doubled the dressing, chopped three smallish apples, and used extra-old sharp white cheddar. Chose a box of Casbah quinoa which cooks in two cups of water without all the palaver of the sieve in the recipe that I followed the first time. I always use dark maple syrup in place of honey in salad dressings which suits this recipe very well. It's a great recipe for a potluck. A keeper, for sure.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Took to a potluck and no one liked it. Just a bad combo of flavors. Raw broccoli was too pungent.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Please answer: The "1 cup quinoa, rinsed" implies the quinoa is a COOKED cup of (rinsed) quinoa. True or not? Those who said this recipe makes more than expected &/or that dressing / other ingredient ratios were off maybe measured the one cup of quinoa before cooking? It would be great to know the intention before I ruin my first attempt at making this.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I agree that it isn't clear (at least to me)! I made this today and used 1 cup uncooked quinoa, which yielded a LOT. So I dumped a hefty amount into the salad and saved some for possible use some other day/way.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I substitute short grain brown rice for the quinoa, and toast slivered almonds instead of pecans. It's delicious, and can't imagine it made any other way!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I cooked this as is except to add some chopped scallions to enhance the flavor. It makes a lot - at least 10-12 side servings.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is the kind of recipe that you can either make as written or use as a springboard to create your very own version(s). Fun, delicious, and nutritious. Perfect for my dietary needs.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I am always looking for healthy travel  food and this is it!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I am a fan of everything that goes into this salad but together they make a mess of conflicting flavors bound together with honey mustard dressing which is cloying.

The salad would be better without the mustard dressing and few less ingredients: a simple 50/50 mix of seasoned rice vinegar and extra virgin olive oil would not mask any of the flavors but add some acidity and it so needs green or red onions to perk it up.  The cheese just gilds this pig’s breakfast.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I really thought this was a horrible recipe. I didn’t like the raw broccoli or the cheddar.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made it exactly as written and it’s incredible! Going into rotation!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I used farrow instead of quinoa and it turned out great, and substituted almonds for pecans because we have a nut allergy, which was decent but did add a crunch

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: We had high hopes for this recipes, but unfortunately, It really disappointed. The flavors don’t meld, and it took almost an hour to prep (not so simple). We thought that it might improve overnight, but it’s just a bland combination of ingredients. I wouldn’t recommend this one.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This salad is delicious! Followed the recipe exactly and it did not disappoint. It came out much larger than I expected, and had to share the wealth with friends.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Used red quinoa.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Oh no!!  What does "season aggressively with salt" mean?  I'm a numbers girl....give me info in teaspoons or tablespoons!  Any suggestions on actual amount of salt?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: To your taste. Honestly, as in all cooking. To your taste.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this and didn’t have cranberries, but subbed a few chopped dates. I also added some celery because my apple lacked the crunch that I hoped it would have. Regardless, this salad was excellent and I hope it becomes a regular staple in our house. It feels fun but still healthy. Thanks, Sohla!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This didn’t do it for me, sadly. It sounds great, no crazy ingredients. Unfortunately, something is missing. It made so much and I’m thinking about tossing it! Any recs on adding flavor? Dressing?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious, but I thought there was too little dressing for these proportions, and I found the Dijon overpowering. I had even added more olive oil and lemon juice than the recipe called for. Next time I'll up both of those and use less mustard. Otherwise, quite yummy.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I added sunflower seeds because I didn’t have pecans.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was tasty but needed a bit more brightness. I think a bit more dressing would do it. I increased the pecans and will add more apple to the leftovers. I did not add cheese. I like a salty thing in salads, but not cheese.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Great salad with lots of protein with the quinoa, pecans and cheddar. Use very sharp cheddar and don’t cut too small. Blanched the broccoli for a few minutes. Would do that again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was quite delicious. I only used the broccoli crowns not the stem. Used feta, walnuts and a mustard vinaigrette that I had on hand. I halved the recipe but it still made easily enough for four.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Stuck to the recipe. It makes a lot more than 6 servings and would be a good addition to a neighborhood pot luck (I'd guess enough for 20 to have a 3/4 cup serving). Not especially memorable, but pleasant enough to make once a year. I used Irish sharp cheddar & a Pink Lady apple.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Really yummy. Probably didn’t need 2tbs of honey as well as the apple and cranberry unless you like it really sweet.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is absolutely delightful, and I could eat it every day. Great for company! I agree that the raw broccoli is “cooked” by the lemon juice, and is not tough or bitter.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I felt that the proportions were way off. I used about half of the sauce ingredients. Cut back on the broccoli and oil. I substituted raisins for cranberries. Also cooked the broccoli. Makes for a good quick meal

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic salad! I followed the recipe as written, allowed it to sit for 3 hours in the refrigerator, and both my partner and I were bowled over how flavorful it was - we ate the entire thing! I did make a little extra vinaigrette for mine, but my better half said his was perfect as is. I might try with toasted almonds on the next go-round. This will be on a regular rotation from now on.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was absolutely delicious! I’m not a raw broccoli fan, but the flavors from the other ingredients really mellowed the strong flavor of broccoli. It’s also the first time I tried quinoa. I used a multicolored variety which made the salad look even more colorful!  Will make this many times.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious, fresh, bright flavours for a bleak winter day. I subbed golden raisins for cranberries and added a grated shallot to the dressing. Will definitely make again!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this as written, although I cut it in half as we are just two people. The one exception was leaving out the cheese. It just didn’t seem to be worth the extra calories for me. My broccoli was chopped smaller than the photo and I agree, don’t par boil it. It was crunchy and delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is delicious, and I look forward to making it for potluck dinners.  I was a bit suspicious of the raw broccoli, but in this recipe it doesn’t taste “raw.”  I even used organic ingredients.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: It is 20 degrees today, I made the quinoa in a larger pot than necessary, then added all ingredients while the quinoa was hot, served lukewarm, what a difference it made on a cold january day!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I used roasted broccoli and almonds instead of pecans. The salad had plenty of crunch from the apples and nuts, and was very flavorful.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this tonight and it was delicious. I changed the lemon to a lime because I didn’t have a lemon. Reduced the apple cider vinegar to 1 tablespoons. Reduced the quinoa to 1/2 cup.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Crunch, bursts of flavors with each bite , balanced , delicious and filling! What’s not to love about this salad?! Definitely adding this to the rotation.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: So I'm doing a pantry challenge for the next two months and adapted this salad to what I has in the house and wanted to use up as well as just preferences. I roasted the broccoli at high heat and think this made a delicious salad even more so. I also subbed walnuts that I also toasted and dried cherries for the cranberries. I also threw in the rest of a jar of a lemony garlic salad dressing I had in the fridge as well as the dressing recipe. I can't stop eating this! Great texture and flavors!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed except I steamed the broccoli for 1 minute first (my digestive tract says no thank you to raw broccoli for some reason), used Pecorino/Romano cheese since I had no cheddar, and use Pistachios since I had no pecans.  Still an outstanding combination of flavors and textures, will definitely keep on my revolving list!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Filled recipe exactly and it was delish!  The broccoli gets “cooked” by the vinegar. I made and stored overnight and then ate. Perfect balance of sweet and tangy.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Followed recipe exactly, let broccoli marinade for about 1/2 hour and texture was perfect.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was very tasting and filling, good for lunches for work. 1 pound of broccoli was sufficient.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: GREAT salad- used candied pecans, oranges for vinaigrette (I didn’t have lemons) and skipped the cheese.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe. I’ve made it a couple of times. Now I leave out the quinoa or any grain because it soaked up the dressing. Fantastic without it! I also sub feta cheese.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Stellar salad; feels both indulgent and virtuous. Made subs based on fridge contents and allergy considerations: 1/2 kale and 1/2 broccoli, dried blueberries, toasted walnuts, and hemp hearts instead of quinoa -- this last one is great because they're thrown in as is, making prep even easier.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious flavor but I found rinsing the quinoa after cooking it made it a bit mushy.  I’d leave out that step next time.  Made with manchego cheese.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I was suspicious when I came across this recipe but when my wife lobbied me to make it I gave in. I ran out of quinoa sono used buckwheat groats instead and was pleasantly surprised. It’s like gourmet reinvented picnic fare. I could make a whole meal out of this.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I zapped the broccoli in the microwave for 2 minutes before it went in the dressing. Just to take the edge off. Really tasty! I’ll cut the broccoli smaller next time.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was REALLY good. The dressing is amazing.  I made a few modifications.....I microwaved the broccoli for a minute or two because I don't like raw broccoli.  I left out the cheese and cranberries, but I'll add them in next time, and there will be a next time!  But even with these changes, it was terrific.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is crazy good…I’ve made it twice in the past week. The broccoli does not taste raw at all. It’s delicious and all of the different textures and tastes are fantastic together. I didn’t have cheddar so I subbed large shavings of Parmesan cheese which was also quite tasty (to me, anyway). Keeps well for a few days though the nuts get a little soft.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent! By a happy mistake I put in the warm quinoa - cooked it per instructions on container and didn't rinse under cold water - but that actually cooked the broccoli a tiny bit.  Will make this again and again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Perfect combination of textures and flavors.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I followed the recipe exactly and it was perfect. Love the broccoli raw…because it’s finely chopped, it’s a perfect crunch; in my opinion, cooking it beforehand, as others have suggested, would have made it mushy. The dressing is delish! I look forward to experimenting with different ingredients!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was a near miss -- the grain was good with the broccoli (used medium bulgur), as were the other ingredients and the dressing, but the totality was not very good. Clark's broccoli salad with garlic and sesame is much better.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The first NYtimes recipe I found lacking. No one in my family would eat salad. Added 3/4 cup of mayo with about 1/4 cup of sugar…..then we all liked it.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made a few substitutions but still turned out great. Couscous and candied walnuts are great, and a quick broccoli boil made it easier to chew. Paired with a salmon fillet and it was a great weeknight dinner.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious! The only changes I made were using sugar-free cranberries and very slightly steaming the broccoli before chopping it.  The dressing is excellent and very flavorful. I'm on the GoLo diet and this has it all:  protein, carbs, vegetables and fat.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This makes a ton! It would probably be fine to make a half recipe. It can certainly serve as the main course.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I'm so into this.  This recipe makes a ton of perfect lunch food for days.

I've made it twice exactly as written except for paring the zest off and then cutting it into tiny chunks instead of grating it; the lemon taste is just a bit more aggressive this way.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Raw broccoli is not my thing, so I roasted it before adding it.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I just made this for lunch and it was delicious. Just trust the raw broccoli -- it works (I already knew this because there is a raw broccoli salad from the WholeFoods deli I like called "broccoli crunch salad"). The dressing is amazing and the mix of textures is perfect. I didn't have pecans, so I used almonds. I'm thinking this would be a great salad to bring to lunch for work. Give this s try!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: As others have noted, this was yummy.  I had to halve the recipe because it's just me.  I happened to have Unexpected Cheddar from Trader Joe's.  As someone suggested, it was wonderful.  The uncooked broccoli was fine.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I followed the recipe exactly except I added more dressing. This is fantastic!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious. Followed the recipe except used peanuts instead of pecans because I am allergic to tree nuts. I will grate the cheese next time like someone suggested.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Unlike some other NYTimes recipes, I think this made more than 4-6 servings. It has a lot of steps, but is easy to make - especially with a food processor - and very tasty. I decreased the mustard a bit, added more apple because I like it and toasted walnuts because that's what I had on hand (and I like them with apples). I'm waiting on the cheese, both because I like the taste as is and because I don't want it to get mushy in the leftovers. I might try adding some tomorrow.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Elaine made for lunch.  Huge recipe - make 1/2.  Use a prairie breeze” cheese - not sharp cheddar.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made as written, and DARN good!!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: We enjoy this exactly as written.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. Uses the Trader Joe’s Aleppo Pepper honey mustard vinaigrette and feta cheese. A , will be making again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I loved this until I added the quinoa. Would make again and omit that entirely — the flavor of the broccoli, nuts, cheddar, cranberries and tangy dressing is very nice and the quinoa just seems to add unneeded bulk

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Followed the exact recipe and it is delicious! It’s flavorful, satisfying, and wholesome —the perfect meal. The raw broccoli was just fine for me.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is a salad I'm actually excited to eat! It has lots of great flavor (the cheese is very important here), though peeling the broccoli can be time-consuming. However, I wouldn't consider it a whole meal. Even after eating a whole bowl, I had to supplement with something else. I may add more quinoa next time.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made for second time. I increased the nuts both times but otherwise, made as outlined. I like how you can use it as a base and add things for variation each time you eat a bowl. I added two fried egg on top for a meal (runny yoke), added mashed avocado, and another time added thick balsamic vinegar w/ fried egg for a punch up of flavor. All delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Even hubby likes it! Sunflower seeds was only variation.  Winner!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I didn't make any improvements in the recipe. It was a hit with the family. Next time I won't make any improvements either!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: BIG success for me! I enjoy the different textures and flavors of all the components. Kind of like a party in my mouth. :-)  I did make a teensy change: I doubled the dressing. As several others have remarked, I’m not a super big fan of raw broccoli, and it was a lot of broccoli when all chopped up. So with the extra dressing as it sat while I prepped and mixed in the rest, the broccoli was softer. Score another one for NYT Food! Thanks!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this exactly according to the recipe and we really liked it. Even my husband who is not usually a broccoli fan enjoyed it.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious even if you cook the broccoli. Husband said no raw broccoli so we blanched it.   Also dividing in half still gives enough for two with a bit of leftovers

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: So so good!!  I made a few swaps - maple syrup (about 1 1/2 TBS) instead of honey, sliced almonds instead of pecans, and farro instead of quinoa…..I will DEFINITELY be making this dish all summer.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Super delicious!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly as described, subbing toasted almonds for pecans ( because it was what I had).  Surprising how quickly the dressing "cooked" the raw broccoli!  Excellent!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: The method for cooking quinoa didn't work for me. After rinsing, I drained in sieve, pushed down a little to extract liquid, and even rolled it up in a kitchen towel to catch more excess liquid. It still had a waterlogged flavor (and I used a lot of salt in the boiling liquid). Added to the salad, it made the whole salad waterlogged tasting. Next time I'll cook quinoa a couple hours in advance the usual way, fluff it, and then let it cool with the lid off.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I don't think raw broccoli is a problem - ever. I chop raw broccoli into green salads for crunch and a bit of a bite on a regular basis. I eat the stem after peeling off the tough outer part and my dogs like it too. So to me this issue with raw broccoli seems odd. We eat raw carrots,  cucumbers, radishes and celery to name a few, so why is broccoli a problem?  BTW, this salad was the bomb!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made a bit extra and wish I'd made even more dressing, although it marinates well over time. Unsweetened cranberries were an excellent choice here, keeping everything nicely balanced and hearty. Delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this as written and wouldn't make it again. It was a weird combination of textures and flavors that just didn't work. Alas, it was quite visually unappealing as well.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and fully worthy of making again with no changes!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this for Cam and Gabe. Makes a ton! Good for potluck.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was quite delicious as-is. No edits made! Others are correct that the acid “cooks” the broccoli so it does not eat raw.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious, used a green apple and followed recipe exactly.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious, makes A LOT. Made as suggested. If cheese is salty, leave out salt in dressing.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Not a fan of cooked broccoli so I was happy to leave it raw. I didn't have pecans so used walnuts. I would perhaps use a little less quinoa next time, although my head of broccoli was small so that might have been the issue. All in all loved this recipe.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: No honey or sugar, added chopped red cabbage, no cheddar, used feta instead

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Very good. I think Sue's comment will help next time.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I didn’t enjoy this until the next day, in my opinion it needed to sit and mellow out. Now I’ll probably make this regularly! Tip: cut up broccoli into tiny florets. I subbed feta for cheddar (it’s all I had) and it came out fine.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Nice combination of ingredients. I always cook/parboil broccoli after having a bad episode of food poisoning from raw broccoli. The dressing is delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious! Hearty and filling, paired beautifully with oven roasted salmon!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was only ok in our house.  “Well, it’s healthy..” was the faint praise from my foodie husband. Adult children took small servings to be polite.  I made it exactly as directed, but added a bit more dressing when it seemed too bland for my tastes.  That really didn’t help much.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I used Ali Saigle recipe (https://cooking.nytimes.com/recipes/1024861-quinoa) for cooking the Quinoa and that made the salad even more delicious, adding a delicate nutty flavor to it.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: When I was at the grocery store and went past the deli counter I realized, YUCK, the salad I'm about to make looks a lot like the gloopy broccoli & cheese salad they sell there that has zero appeal. Still, I made it anyway, equating mass-produced anything to be far inferior to homemade. While the ingredients weren't exactly the same as the store-made salad, this just didn't work for me, my husband, or sister. I cut the vinegar to 1 tbsp, as I'm not a huge fan, but it just didn't work. Pass.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Endlessly adaptable!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Good idea but not great. Lightly steam broccoli. Too sweet. Use less quinoa and consider toasting it

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I think the recipe as written was fine but somewhat bland. The next day I added crispy bacon and it was super. (Figured it was already not the healthiest dish given all the cheese, so why not?) Also, next time I make this I’ll try toasting the quinoa as one reviewer suggested.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I don’t know. I really wanted to like this but it was just bland. I know I used a little more broccoli than the recipe but I also increased the dressing and even added some chili sauce. Not a fan.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Not that delicious.  With so many stars I was expecting more flavor.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: DELICIOUS, make it just as written. Easy to substitute if you wish later (I prefer dates to dried cranberries) but trust that this dressing brings it all together beautifully.  Even better the next day.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I bought a bag of broccoli florets and steamed them in bag for a couple mins just to soften them. Easy and they were already cut up. I didn’t cook them through just a little bit. Otherwise recipe exactly as written. Delicious!!!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this as written (with 50% sugar dried cranberries - we don’t notice a difference) and pulled all but the cooked quinoa together. Perfection! Served the quinoa on the side to meet family preferences. A delicious combination all around that will be made again. Benefitted from the juice of the full lemon.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Really good. I skipped the cheese.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Wonderful, bright flavor. Ir was a hit.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: So fresh and tangy! I don’t generally care for raw broccoli as some others commented here. But the dressing truly softens it. In fact, I doubled the dressing for extra flavor. I didn’t have dried cranberries on hand but I always have a couple bags of frozen and so I diced up about 2/3 cup and tossed those in. I also took the time to toast my pecans. This is definitely going to be on repeat and on the potluck list, as well.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I thought this was delicious but then I again, I added bacon…. So my results are highly skewed

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: made as written, perfectly delicious!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Wonderful salad!  I used pomegranates instead of the dried cranberries, added a nice crunch and flavor.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is one of the yummiest salads I've ever made. It's a lot of chopping but worth the effort. Way too much quinoa  tho - could probably half that and despite everyone's skepticism(including my husband's) the uncooked broccoli  is the right call.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Do you really have to rinse the cooked quinoa? I don’t have a sieve and wondered if it was necessary and why?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I accidentally measured 3tbsps of apple cider vinegar, but it tasted great to me so would probably do that on purpose. Letting the broccoli marinate in the dressing while prepping the other ingredients took the raw edge off of it for me. I did cut it up pretty small, which might have helped. I liked the cheddar, but am skeptical of how well it will hold up in the dressing after a day or two.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this as is, with the broccoli raw. It turned out great. chopped dried cherries, in lieu of cranberries, which worked well. Made it without cheese and it was excellent then added some TJ‘s unexpected cheddar. I’m sure this would also be great with Parmesan. Sending this round to all my friends who cook and like to eat healthy.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: When does the zest come in?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made this with farro instead of quinoa. Used my food processor to chop the apples and broccoli. Easy and absolutely delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: So very good!! Made as written.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
One of my pet peeves is recipes with instructions to “salt to taste”.  Salting a dish is so important. A good recipe gives the reader a sense of how much salt is reasonable and he/she then can adjust to use more or less the amount.  This recipe is probably the worst “season aggressively with salt”?!!!  What the heck?  NYT, please please request your recipe developers to add specific measurements for salt.  Something like “1 teaspoon of Kosher salt or to taste” would be very helpful.  This is why I like Ina Garten’s recipes.  She gives specific measurements for everything including salt. 

Now on to the recipe itself, I like raw broccoli and quinoa and all ingredients used here.  However, I found in combination it’s only okay, not spectacular.  Id make it again but I’d leave out the cheese and just add it when I feel like it.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: made this today and it exceeded expectations. definilyy don’t boil broccoli- just chop it up and it gives a great crunch. added sweet potatoe and that tied everything together for me and made it a hearty meal! i took out nuts and cranberries because i don’t like them and it worked perfectly

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious.  I can eat this for lunch for days and days.  I think it is much prettier with red quinoa!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I added juice from 1 1/2 lemons and I’m not sorry.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I was mistrustful of using raw broccoli but followed advise from a commenter and glad I did.  It was fabulous 
I was also mistrustful of using 3 Tbs of Dijon so used 1 Tbs. Next time I’ll go up to 1 1/2 Tbs.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is fab! Subbed pomegranate molasses for the honey but otherwise followed the recipe (broccoli does not need to be cooked). A good use for the bags and bags of pecans in my freezer from my mom’s tree in Texas.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this following the recipe. Everyone loved it. Based on several comments, I almost parboiled the broccoli but decided not to. I'm glad I didn't. Next time, I would add a little less quinoa to enjoy a little more flavor from the dressing.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I can't eat dairy and have not found a good dairy-free alternative to sharp cheddar. Anyone here found something?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Excellent! Made as written except I substituted farro because I'm not a big fan of quinoa. I live alone, but made the entire recipe since it will keep well and I'll add other proteins to it for variety.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Yes!  Had to use a mix of cauliflower and broccoli and cilantro instead of parsley and feta vs cheddar to avoid last minute market run.  Still awesome!  For leftovers we keep adding more cheese (cheddar this time) and cranberries.  It’s the dressing that pulls it together.  Went liberal with the lemons.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Quite nice. Used what I jad i the hose which meant Havarti instead of cheddar.  Think the cheddar would add more interest. Generous with the pecans and cranberries'. Will be perfect summertime dish.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I added some thinly sliced red onions and let them marinate in the dressing while I got the rest of the ingredients together. They added nice flavor and texture!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: recipe is a keeper. I made it as written, then tasted it and decided to double the dressing, which was a good idea. I used red quinoa, because that’s what I had, and I included the cubed cheddar, but it would have been great without it. To the commenters worried about raw broccoli - trust the process. You won’t be sorry. I served this last night alongside a roast chicken and my husband said, “this would be great itself in a wrap for lunch,” so that’s what we will do with the leftovers.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Great, easy salad. I tossed it and let it sit for an hour so the broccoli could soak in the vinegar a bit. It was perfect for a weeknight meal. 

Notes: I only used 1/2 cup of pecan bits and wish I had done 3/4. I wish I had used slightly less than the prescribed amount of water for the quinoa. I don't love a ton of dressing and this was the perfect amount for me. I didn't add cranberries because I don't like them.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this a few times. It’s super satisfying and makes me look forward to lunches for the week! My only change is that I add a couple heaping spoonfuls of chili crisp to the dressing. So good!!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: So good and so simple! Agree with everyone - leave broc raw like the recipe says. Chop it in small pieces, and the vinegar lemon juice dressing will soften it up nicely. I didn’t have dried cranberries on hand so I used raisins instead, and I left out the mustard because I’m not a fan. Also left out the cheese - seems unnecessary for a salad like this, but I might add it if/when I make this for a potluck.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: love  raw broccoli@Sue

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I dislike raw broccoli but it was really good in this salad. I marinated the chopped broccoli in the dressing a few hours ahead. I used bulgur instead of quinoa and that worked well. I think this would be an excellent Thanksgiving side for vegetarians.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was shocking it's so good. I want to eat it every day. It lasts in the fridge for a week. Not quite as good as the first day, but still yummy

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: It was the oddly paired ingredients that drew me to try this recipe.  Wow!  Just delicious.  Will add to my favorite ways to sneak broccoli into my diet.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is a staple in my kitchen, although I only make mine with Farro rather than quinoa. Everyone who eats it raves about it, it’s easy to make, substitutions are plentiful, allowing me to use what I have in my fridge or pantry, if necessary. I can’t find anything to complain about!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’m terribly allergic to mustard so miss out on wonderful vinaigrettes like this one. What can I substitute?

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
The salad makes 6 servings, with 455 calories per serving, with 25 grams of fat and 6 grams of saturated fat per serving; so fat is about 49.5% of total calories.

Has anyone tried it without the olive oil and cheddar cheese? From the ingredients, it looks like it would still be very tasty, and much lower in fat. And I liked the suggestion to blanche the broccoli first.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: @Dr. J Yes. Cheddar is not necessary. I also made the quinoa crispy & added at the last minute. Just don’t leave out the lemon - it is key. Delicious

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Quite delicious!  I did not have quinoa, but I did have some leftover brown rice. It was satisfying with the brocolli salad.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Used hot honey in the dressing.  YUM!!!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I was skeptical of the raw broccoli but I bought some very fresh from the farmer’s market and chopped it finely as if I was dicing onions. It was tender and really didn’t need cooking. Absolutely delicious and a great potluck or picnic dish!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I left out the cheddar & did not miss it. I also made the quinoa crispy in the oven & added it just before serving. This salad is delicious. It keeps well so you can enjoy it for days.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I added some celery and substituted brussels sprouts for broccoli and it was quite good.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Super yummy - a great bring to a potluck or picnic as it makes  a lot.  However, since finely chopped broccoli is NOT one  of the ingredients, beware - it will take 30 minutes to clean, peel and finley chop them for step 3.  NYT timing is really off here, taking you way beyond the 30 minutes prep time listed.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Sure, add as much stuff as possible till the taste of broccoli.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I used the Trader Joe’s farro- added about 2 cups cooked. Was great and made it hearty and more appealing to my spouse. 
This is excellent, balanced with the juice from the lemon at the end, and so much black pepper. Yum.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I always know Melissa Clark recipes are going to be good. Deep flavor, varied texture and nice acidity in this without needing much time to prepare. Plus it makes lots of leftovers for lunches.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I happened to make this recipe this week but used frozen broccoli because that is what I had.

The dressing was a little sweet for me and I reduced the honey by half but overall doubled the dressing.

Because I am on a very low sodium diet, I used low sodium mustard, did not add any additional salt, and left out the cheese.

Very good recipe.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I love this.  Easy and delicious.  I made it as per the recipe, other than adding in some diced orange pepper I needed to use up.  Wouldn't change a thing!  And I agree that parboiling the broccoli isn't needed here.  The broccoli is cut small and the dressing softens it up already so it's not as crunchy as if you were just eating raw broccoli.  

Will definitely make this again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this using wheat berries instead of quinoa and feta instead of cheddar. It was a hit. The dressing completely tenderized the broccoli. It was a hit and I will definitely make it on a regular basis.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Feta! Yes, I was hesitant about cheddar myself.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Thoughts on switching out broccoli for Brussel sprouts? It is what I have on hand.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
I make something similar with quinoa, black beans, red bell pepper, corn, parsley, and red onion. Of course you can mix it up by adding or substituting garbanzos or whatever sounds good.

Pro tip: If you make it (or this NYT recipe) WITHOUT any kind of dressing, it keeps a long time in the fridge. Just add when eating. Yes, as this recipe notes, with the vinaigrette it gets better for a while, but it also greatly reduces its shelf life (stated at 3 days). The one I make can go almost 2 weeks.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I didn’t have enough broccoli and added some shaved Brussels sprouts and sugar snap peas that I had on hand. Used crumbled goat cheese. It was delicious and I thought even better the next day. A keeper for sure.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I was skeptical of this salad, but oh my goodness it is delicious! All of the flavors go well together and the broccoli softens from the citrus and apple cider vinegar. The pecans add a crunchy texture and nutty flavor. It also made more than 4-6 servings, so now I have more to eat throughout the week!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Regardless of preparation, I've never found quinoa to be edible. It has a nasty taste that no amount of rinsing seems to wash away. Many things that people turn their noses up have been delicious in my experience, but not this. The idea of substituting wild rice sounds good and I'll give that a shot.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. I made it as directed and will make it again. And again.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Definitely no need to Blanche the broccoli, crisp and crunch is what this salad is all about

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and riffable. Used goat cheese bc I had it (but definitely want to try cheddar). Raw broccoli taste was too much for my taste, even with dressing and after sitting a day or two. Next time I'll put broccoli in the strainer and drain the grain over it, or drop it in the pot just before draining. The quinoa kind of disappeared - farro or bulgar might work better here. I'll also try unsweetened dried tart cherries - I love them as an alternative to sugar-laden dried cranberries.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Additional notes... As others noted, the recipe makes a LOT - I've had it 4 days in a row and still have probably 1/3 of it left. Next time I'll halve it (or make it for a group). As written, dressing amount is just enough. If you prefer a "dressier" salad, you might want to make a little extra. Also, the pecans get soft in the fridge. If you're likely to have leftovers, hold the pecans to top individual servings so you don't lose the great crunch and toasty flavor.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Made it today. As others have mentioned, I also had 1.5 lbs of broccoli florets.... After I faithfully chopped it all up -  the resulting amount was ... frightening.. Still, I followed the dressing recipe exactly, thought it was inadequate for the mountain of broccoli... Added just about 2/3 of quinoa... Mixed in all the other ingredients except nut.. I was expecting some Miracle to occur ...
As I had expected, the resulting mix was pedestrian.. I've added a bit of garam masala to perk it up..

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Meh. It has too many ingredients and together they yield a salad struggling between sweet & salty. It reminded me of the gloppy hippie salads one person always brings to a potluck. Next!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Try with twice the lemon and half the honey

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: best. cheese. ever. Great call @Em !

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is amazing. I made as directed ( added a small shallot, cuz why not). Family loved it, not as many leftovers as hoped for.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
This is truly fantastic! I made it exactly as written, although I was momentarily daunted by 
"season aggressively with salt," so I decided to start "timidly" with a rounded half-teaspoon, which was fine to my taste. I might suggest changing the number of servings to 6-8, especially if you serve this alongside something else. That said, this salad is soooo good that second helpings are likely.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Really good salad! Served it at brunch along with a heavy egg dish. Our guests loved it. Appreciate that it’s naturally gluten free. I’m not a fan of raw broccoli but this works. We made it yesterday and chilled overnight. Perfect!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Blanch the broccoli stems! I used Cabot gouda and chopped dates in it since that's what I had and YUM. Great lunch but also great side dish with some pan-seared pork chops & a little extra mustard vinaigrette.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Fabulous salad!!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I did not think this was going to be that good based on the ingredients but OMG, you really should try this. It is absolutely delicious. 5*

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Too much going on in this salad. And not enough pay off in taste. I ended up throwing it out. And that was a lot of food wasted!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I was a little doubtful when I decided to try this but we really liked it.  I cut the quinoa back to 3/4 cup and used a bit less broccoli but it still made a big bowlful. Had it with dinner and several lunches

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: When I read this recipe, I thought “Gosh, these things shouldn’t go together.” Made the dish and proved myself correct.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this recipe a few times and it doesn’t fail. Colby Jack worked perfectly this time. This dish is comfort food to me and it’s a brilliant alternative whenever I’m craving something “nasty” (like fast food). It somehow amuses the child in me —who’s always loved broccoli— and this makes me want to cook it for my nephew.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This salad is bright, fresh, and satisfying. I reduced the olive oil by 1 tablespoon, and used juice from whole lemon. Because I chopped the broccoli and apple in small pieces, the fully mixed salad fit nicely on the spoon. Salad is perfect for the warm weather.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. I used buckwheat groats instead of quinoa and feta cheese instead of cheddar. The broccoli was softened (by the sauce?), didn't seem raw. The whole family loved it including the septuagenarians and the toddler. It's now on our weekly rotation.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Add chick peas

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Half the recipe was the right amount for 2 people and to have left overs. It took 45 minutes to make. It took longest to trim/cut the broccoli into little bit size pieces. No need to chop the broccoli after that.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Much better using asparagus and Feta cheese

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly as written. Wanted to like it - I like each of the ingredients and I liked the dressing but together it just didn't seem to work for me or my husband.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is a winner! I missed the note about chopping the broccoli finely but even with larger pieces I would not recommend parboiling as some other reviewers have suggested. Made a few subs based on what I had on hand - farro instead of quinoa, almonds instead of pecans and I left out the cheese to make it dairy free. Super delicious and I can tell it's going to be even better tomorrow!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: A bowl of utter bliss! Seriously, the best raw veggie salad I have ever made, and I've made a lot. I cut the amount of quinoa (half a cup was all I found in the pantry), and honestly, I think I'll do it that way next time, too. Perfect. Also, next time, I think I might omit the cheese. Didn't really seem necessary.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I omitted the cheese and used currants instead of cranberries and short grain brown rice instead of quinoa. Delicious!  The raw broccoli and apple give this salad a great texture.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I too was scared of raw broccoli so I roasted it a bit. I also left out the cheese because it's everywhere and I'm trying to eat less. And I cut back on some of the quinoa. Liked it better than I thought I would and will try it with the raw broccoli next time. Definitely will also play around with it. The dressing is really good.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: A bit fussy for us with all the fine chopping, which indeed came out nice, but next time we’ll probably be more lax about that part and I’m sure it’ll still be delicious.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: What is this amazingness?!?!  Made the recipe without substitutions and as directed. Highly recommend.  It makes a big portion so can feed for days or great for a gathering.  I see many seasons in this salad...might even be amazing with some crunchy bacon but that could just take it over the edge.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody:
Y’all give the raw broccoli a shot. It makes the texture of the entire salad.
I only had the florets so it was a bit annoying to get rid of the rough skin. I would add more lemon next time

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Love this recipe! I use the dressing for everything now

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious and makes a lot!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made this with 1 1/2 # of broccoli as recommended, and it was enough servings for at least 12 people. I doubled the dressing, and it was still too dry. I think maybe a parboil on the broccoli could help...

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Late to the party but wanted to chime in to agree with everyone who said that this was scrumptious, that you can sub nuts, that you do not need to cook the broccoli, and that it took longer than the time suggested. All in all, a revelation of a salad!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I left out the cheddar cheese - not needed.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was a great recipe.  In the rotation now. With the exception of fish and eggs, my diet looks more like that of plant-based.  I added chopped chick peas (I'm not a big chick pea lover, hence I chop them up and mix in!) for added protein and used more pecans and dried cranberries ...because I just like them!  Thanks for a great recipe.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious as is.  We made it into a main dish salad by adding 2 tins of drained, rinsed and dried black beans.  Next time I add beans I will double the dressing.  Without cheese (or honey for strict vegans), it is delicious vegan meal.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!   Hubby agreed.   I didn’t have any apples, so substituted a few dried apricots for sweetness, and a bunch of chopped up French breakfast radishes for crunch.  Also, since it is Springtime, I included some chopped up garlic chives!   Agree it is too much g quinoa, so used the extra later.  Nice flavors and varying textures make this a very interesting salad!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: This was so good. We all loved it. I ended up using barley because that’s what I had and it worked out great. Will definitely keep this one around.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: Excellent! Made this for a potluck at Jitendra & Ellen's; everyone liked it. Don't double the recipe; it's too hard to mix that much quinoa and broccoli. It makes enough to serve 8-10 as is.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I really enjoyed this, but it needs to be eaten pretty quickly. It makes a lot so be prepared for that.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this twice now because I want to love it, but the combination of ingredients just didn’t work for me. It was weird and just okay. It results in a huge quantity, which we likely won’t finish.

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I made as directed last year, loved it and reviewed it, but tonight I tried a different version of my own. With yellow zucchini fresh from the garden instead of broccoli, quartered, sliced, grilled in grill pan and added to the warm quinoa. Omitted apple, nuts and cheese. Added julienned basil, halved cherry tomatoes, and sliced Klamath olives. For the dressing, omitted honey and used balsamic instead of apple cider vinegar. Very Mediterranean and a nice riff!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
commentBody: I really enjoyed this. Unfortunately I chopped the ingredients a little too finely to really taste any one specifically, but the texture is lovely with a nice chew.  It makes A LOT!

author: Sohla El-Waylly
recipeTitle: Quinoa and Broccoli Spoon Salad Recipe
